_doc: Autoritativní per-fórum profily. Když profil existuje, calibrateForum ho použije PŘÍMO (naseeduje sekce + selektory + parser, calibration_status='calibrated') a PŘESKOČÍ automatickou LLM kalibraci. Pro fóra, která auto-kalibrace nezvládne (custom enginy, netypické URL vláken). Sepiš jednou důkladně; agent pak jen čte. Pole: match (doména nebo předpona URL), engine, language, brands, sections[] (plné URL technických sekcí), calibration{post_selector, header_selector?, author_selector?, content_selector?, date_selector?, quote_selector?, thread_url_pattern?, thread_list_selector?, pagination_selector?, max_section_pages?, force_browser?, cookie?}, notes. POZOR: fórum, které už je ve stavu 'calibration_failed', nový profil samo nezvedne — je nutné ho resetovat (node scripts/agent/reset-forum.mjs) nebo přidat nové, aby ho calibrate phase znovu vzala.

profiles.name: BMW-Syndikat
profiles.notes: Legacy ASP fórum (Snitz-like), DIV skin, server-rendered (bez JS), bez loginu. Vlákna: /bmwsyndikatforum/topic{N}_{titulek}.html (bez lomítka za 'topic' → generický isLikelyThreadUrl je nepozná, proto thread_url_pattern). Sekce: forum{N}_*.html. SPECIFIKUM: autor (span.forum_show_headline1 a) je v hlavičce div.headline_big_show TĚSNĚ PŘED tělem div.forum_show_outer — proto header_selector. Zpráva je v div.forum_show_main_top (mimo postranní panel a signaturu). Šum (reklama 'Ad-Boy', box 'Ähnliche Beiträge' s prázdným autorem) extrakce ignoruje. Kódování ISO-8859-1 (umlauty zkomolené, neblokuje). force_browser NENÍ potřeba.

profiles.name: AudiDrivers.de — 'DAS' Audi Forum
profiles.notes: phpBB3 with proglass skin. Reachable via plain fetch (no browser). Standard viewtopic.php thread URLs match the generic filter, so no thread_url_pattern needed. The built-in phpBB engine parser FAILS to extract authors (it returns mostly empty authors and includes 'von » ' header noise in the text). Custom selectors fix this: author lives in p.author as 'von USERNAME' (p.author strong captures the clean username, incl. username-coloured ones), and content is in div.content. Verified across two technical sections (Motor f=10 thread t=4523, Elektrik f=32 thread t=2848) — all posts parse with non-empty authors and clean German text. Technical sections selected: Motor(10), Turbo(37), Antrieb(29), Fahrwerk/Räder(11), Bremsen(30), Klima/Heizung/Lüftung(31), Elektrik/Elektronik/Licht(32), Werkzeuge/Werkstatt/OBD(42), OnBoard-Diagnose OBD(44). Excluded marketplace/news/off-topic/meetups (Marktplatz f=3, off topic f=8, Treffen f=6/15, gewerbliche Angebote etc.).

profiles.name: Audi-Sport.net
profiles.notes: Standard XenForo 2.x at /xf/. Plain fetch returns full HTML (~366KB), no browser or login needed. XenForo engine parser extracts posts with non-empty authors and clean text out of the box (verified on multiple threads). Standard /xf/threads/N/ URLs already match the generic filter, so no thread_url_pattern needed. Sections = General Technical/How To (105), VCDS diagnostics (27), Diesel (28), Tuning (63), Handling (62), Air Conditioning (137), plus all active model-specific chassis subforums (A3/S3, A4/S4, A5/S5, A6/S6, RS3/RS4/RS6, A7/A8, Q-series, R8, TT, Classic). Excluded marketplace/events/off-topic/vendor subforums. Most chassis forums use numeric IDs; a few legacy ones use slug URLs (not included since numeric equivalents cover the active models).

profiles.name: Audizine Audi Enthusiast Forums
profiles.notes: XenForo on VerticalScope. Standard XenForo parser extracts posts+authors+clean text with NO custom selectors (verified 13 posts on brake-upgrades DIY thread and 24 posts on B8 A4 thread, all with distinct non-empty authors). Thread URLs are standard /threads/slug.ID/ (covered by generic filter, no thread_url_pattern needed). IMPORTANT: do NOT set force_browser. Plain HTTP is blocked by VerticalScope WAF (403/406), but fetchHtml auto-escalates to the fingerprinted Crawlee fallback which succeeds; the forceBrowser system-Chrome path actually FAILS against this WAF. Sections cover model-line forums (where fault/repair threads live), cross-model engine-tech subforums (1.8T/2.0T/V6/V8), and dedicated technical forums (forced induction, drivetrain, brakes/suspension, air suspension, car audio/OEM electrical, DIY archive). Excluded: classifieds, regional/local, news, intros, off-topic, feedback. Note many engine-tech subforums are duplicated per model via /link-forums/ aliases pointing to the same content with different IDs; representative ones included.

profiles.name: TorqueCars Car Forums
profiles.notes: Standard XenForo. Plain fetch returns full HTML (no browser, no anti-bot). Engine parser extracts posts+authors+text cleanly — no custom selectors needed. Standard /forums/threads/<slug>.<id>/ URLs match the generic filter, so no thread_url_pattern needed. Sections are the technical/engine/tuning/drivability subforums; marketplace/news/meets/owners-club-chat excluded.

profiles.name: Volvo Owners Club
profiles.notes: vBulletin 5.7.5 (generator confirms). Engine detect says 'generic'; the agent's vbulletin parser targets vB4 markup and returns 0 posts here, so use engine=generic with the custom selectors below. Modern vB5 markup: post container .b-post, author in div.author (anchor->span[itemprop=name]), body in div.js-post__content-text. Verified on two threads in different sections (ECM-6805/boost in S80/S60/V70 general, and EGR problems in Diesel Engines): both parsed 15/15 posts with non-empty authors and clean text. Plain fetch returns full usable HTML (~180KB), no browser needed. Sections are model-specific 'General' subforums plus topic forums (Diesel, AWD, LPG, Performance, Towing); their child *-sales/*-wants/*-articles subforums are marketplace/howto and were intentionally excluded. Thread URLs are /forum/<path>/<numericID>-<slug>; pattern added to be safe though /5-digit-slug also resembles standard. min_post_length default 40 would drop very short one-line replies — acceptable, keeps higher-value posts.

profiles.name: VWVortex
profiles.notes: XenForo (signatures + /forums/<slug>.N/ and /threads/<slug>.N/ URLs; standard, generic thread filter matches — no thread_url_pattern needed). EN. Engine parser parses posts WITH authors and clean text — no custom selectors needed. ANTI-BOT: VerticalScope proof-of-work interstitial served at HTTP 200 as a ~1.8KB JS shell (window.POW_CHALLENGE_DATA / challenge_nonce). Its headless_check refuses the cookie when navigator.webdriver=true, so the system-Chrome --dump-dom (force_browser) path CANNOT reliably solve it (section/listing pages fail 'no usable HTML'; only the index + some thread pages slip through). DO NOT set force_browser. The fingerprinted Crawlee browser sets navigator.webdriver=false and clears the PoW (~4-5s), returning full HTML for both section listings and threads. REQUIRED CODE FIX (applied): added /POW_CHALLENGE_DATA/i and /challenge_nonce/i to BROWSER_FETCH_PATTERNS in scripts/agent/fetch-utils.mjs so the plain-fetch path detects the PoW shell and escalates to Crawlee; without it isLikelyChallengeHtml returned false and the agent kept the 1811-byte shell (0 threads). After the fix the recon section command returns 113 thread-like URLs and parse returns posts+authors via the plain path. Crawlee/Playwright must be installed (it is). Sections are model-specific (Golf/GTI/Jetta/Passat/Tiguan/etc) + cross-cutting engine/transmission/diagnostic/electrical forums; marketplace/cars/parts/regional/lounge/news omitted.

profiles.name: MeinID.com — VW ID Forum
profiles.notes: WoltLab (Burning Board). Reachable via plain fetch, no browser needed. Engine parser extracts authors fine but its default content selector is noisy (pulls user sidebar: rank/Reaktionen/Punkte/Beiträge/Fahrzeug stats). Custom author_selector=.messageAuthor .username and content_selector=.messageText give clean authors + clean message body (signatures live in a separate .messageFooter and are excluded). Post selector left to engine default (.wbbPost works). Threads use standard /thread/N-slug/ — matched by generic filter, no thread_url_pattern needed. Each thread contains one injected WoltLab ad post (author 'AdMan' via itemprop=name, not .username) which the author_selector naturally yields as empty so downstream extraction skips it.

profiles.name: RenaultForum.net
profiles.notes: WoltLab Suite 6 (Burning Board). German Renault community. Plain fetch returns full usable HTML (~446KB), no browser needed. WoltLab engine parser finds posts but text is noisy (grabs header metadata) and guest authors come back empty; custom selectors required: post=.wbbPost, author=.messageAuthor .username (works for both registered users and guests), content=.messageText (clean message body). Standard /board/N-slug/ section and /thread/N-slug/ thread URLs — engine handles thread-link detection (44-45 thread links per section page), no thread_url_pattern needed. Sections list covers per-model Elektronik/Mechanik/Multimedia plus Laguna3 Mängel & Lösungen; marketplace/Suche/Biete, Optik & Tuning, Reifen & Felgen, Teile & Zubehör, off-topic/news/meetup boards intentionally excluded.

profiles.name: Audi Club EU
profiles.notes: Custom Czech CMS (not a standard forum engine; detect=generic). Boards live under /forum (index lists all). Threads = /forum-tema/<slug>-<id> (non-standard, must set thread_url_pattern). Categories = /forum-kategorie/<model>-<id>; 85 boards, mostly per Audi model/generation + a dedicated 'technicky-koutek' (Technical Corner). Each post is a TR id=messageNNNN inside table.forum_posts; author in a.show_user_box, body in the post's P. Content is CZ and SK mixed (many SK posters) — primary site lang cs. Static HTML, no browser needed, reading needs no login. Minor noise: occasional partner/ad row parses as an author-less post (e.g. 'Nezaplatim.cz Partner klubu') — filter empty-author posts downstream. Site is active (threads dated 2025-12). /poradna is a separate Q&A area (poradna-dotaz), not the main board.

profiles.name: BMW Club CZ
profiles.notes:
Custom PHP engine (autoclub.eu platform), not a standard forum engine - detect.mjs reports generic. Czech-language BMW club, anonymous read access (no login wall). Board index at /forum; per-chassis technical boards live under /forum-kategorie/-. Threads under /forum-tema/- (NON-standard, needs thread_url_pattern). Section pagination via ?kols=N, ~20 threads/page; large boards (E90 = 141 pages). Posts in table.forum_posts as ; author in div.div_user a.show_user_box; body in trailing

(content_selector:p strips author-name line, timestamp and button artifacts). One injected sponsor/ad per thread has empty author + null id and will be dropped naturally. 92 total forum-kategorie sections exist; profiled a representative high-traffic subset (mainstream 1/3/5/7/X/Z chassis + technicky-koutek + navody) - omitted off-topic/marketplace boards (vtipy, pokec, srazy, inzerce, vip-arena, ukradena-vozidla, dopravni-prestupky). Plain HTTP fetch works, no browser needed.

profiles.name: Citroën klub (Citroen Club CZ)
profiles.notes:
Custom CZ/SK Citroen+DS club CMS (PHP, server-rendered plain HTML; detect.mjs=generic, no engine signature). Reachable, no login required to READ threads (login.php/register.php exist only for posting). Boards live under /forum-kategorie/-; threads under /forum-tema/- (non-standard, hence thread_url_pattern). Posts are rows tr[id^=message] inside table.forum_posts; author link a.show_user_box (also appears on avatar but selectPosts takes first), message body in

tags (content=td p strips the author+timestamp prefix that a bare td selector picked up). One non-user 'div.partner' advertiser row appears per thread inside the same table and parses with an EMPTY author -> the agent's non-empty-author filter drops it. Content is CZ and SK mixed (typical for this club). force_browser NOT needed. Sections list = all model-specific boards + Technicky koutek + Navody; excluded off-topic/marketplace/meetups (pokec, tuning, dopravni-prestupky, ukradena-vozidla, klubove-zalezitosti, o-srazech, o-clancich, skupiny, vtipy). DS6/older niche boards (AX/BX/CX/ZX/XM/C-Crosser/C-Zero etc.) omitted from list for brevity but follow the same pattern if needed.

profiles.name: Club Opel
profiles.notes: Custom Czech autoclub.eu CMS, not standard forum software — detect.mjs returns generic, no signatures. Reachable over static HTML, no login or JS challenge (force_browser not needed). Reading public; only posting/PM needs login. Board index /forum lists model categories /forum-kategorie/<model>-<id> (Astra/Corsa/Vectra/Zafira/Insignia/Omega/Meriva/Mokka/Signum etc.) plus cross-model technicky-koutek-6 and navody-9 — the technical/fault sections; skipped marketplace (inzerce), news (clanky), meetups (srazy), off-topic (pokec-vseobecny, vtipy), insurance, stolen-vehicle boards. Threads are /forum-tema/<slug>-<id> (non-standard, hence thread_url_pattern=/forum-tema/). CZ with frequent SK posts mixed. Generic engine parser yields 0 posts so custom selectors required: posts are <tr id='messageNNNNN'> rows in table.forum_posts; author=a.show_user_box link; body=td > p (clean diagnostic text). td>p also matches occasional embedded partner/ad rows with empty author — sparse, easily filtered. Active: live 2026-04 threads on DPF regeneration, engine cut-out, cooling fans, OpCom diagnostics. High value for Opel RAG.

profiles.name: Dacia Club CZ
profiles.notes:
Custom CZ autoclub.eu platform (detect=generic, no standard engine; generic parser yields 0 posts -> custom selectors required). Static HTML, no login to read, no JS/browser needed. Board: /forum -> categories /forum-kategorie/- -> threads /forum-tema/-, paginated ?kols=N. Posts are table rows TR[id^=message]; author link .show_user_box + timestamp +

body share one TD; content_selector=P strips author/timestamp noise. Dedicated 'zjistene zavady' (detected faults) threads per model = high RAG value. Excluded /inzerce (marketplace), /srazy & o-srazech (meetups), vtipy (jokes), pokec-vseobecny (off-topic), klubove-zalezitosti.

profiles.name: Ford Club EU (fordclub.eu)
profiles.notes:
Custom CZ PHP CMS (not a standard engine; detect.mjs=generic, no engine signatures). Board index at /forum; categories at /forum-kategorie/-; threads at /forum-tema/-. Custom thread_url_pattern needed because /forum-tema/ is not matched by the generic thread filter. Posts are rows inside table.forum_posts; author is the first a.show_user_box (username) in div.div_user; content_selector=p grabs the

body and excludes the user/buttons boxes. Each thread also has ~1 injected partner-ad row that yields an empty author and is dropped downstream. Public read access, no login. force_browser NOT needed (plain fetch works).

profiles.name: Ford Transit Forum
profiles.notes: Standard prosilver phpBB. Real board is under /forum/ (viewforum.php / viewtopic.php). The default phpbb engine parser fails here: it leaves authors empty for non-admin members (only admins carry .username-coloured) and mixes 'Reply with quote' + date noise into text, so custom selectors are required. post=.post, author='p.author strong a' (username link in the byline, works for both coloured admin and plain member links), content=.content (clean body, quotes preserved). Thread URLs are standard viewtopic.php?t=… so no thread_url_pattern needed; generic filter matches them. No login required, no JS/browser needed (plain fetch works). Sections chosen are technical: f=2 Technical Problems & Questions, f=18 Technical How-to, plus model-generation boards f=3 Mk1&2, f=4 Mk3-5, f=5 Mk6&7, f=64 Mk8, f=65 Transit Custom, f=54 Transit Connect. Marketplace/wanted/gallery/events/off-topic boards excluded. Forum is large and active: thread IDs into the 227k range, posts spanning 2006 through 2026; member 'Luke' (Site Admin) has 44k+ posts.

profiles.name: Mercedes Club CZ
profiles.notes:
Custom CMS (generator/none; table.forum_posts skin), not a standard engine. Posts = ; author = first a.show_user_box; body =

; date = . Threads at /forum-tema/- (non-standard -> thread_url_pattern required). Section listings paginate via ?kols=N and link threads as /forum-tema/...; ignore ?select=#message deep-links. One harmless partner-ad row (div.partner) parses with empty author and is dropped by the same-author validation gate. Plain HTTP fetch works; no browser/login needed.

profiles.name: Nissan Club CZ
profiles.notes:
Custom autoclub.eu CMS (no standard forum engine signatures). Sections are per-model boards at /forum-kategorie/-; pagination via ?kols=N. Threads at /forum-tema/- (non-standard -> thread_url_pattern needed). Posts live in table.forum_posts rows tr[id^=message]; author in a.show_user_box, content in 2nd TD (date span +

paragraphs). Mostly CZ with some SK posts (CZ/SK club). No login needed to read.

profiles.name: Peugeot Club EU (Peugeot klub CZ/SK)
profiles.notes:
Custom Czech PHP CMS (not a standard engine). Boards organized per Peugeot model under /forum-kategorie/-, plus dedicated technicky-koutek (technical corner) and navody (how-to). Threads live at /forum-tema/- (non-standard, hence thread_url_pattern). Posts are rows in table.forum_posts; author = first a.show_user_box in div_user; message body =

inside the TD (content=td also works but pulls in author/timestamp/button noise, so content=p is cleaner). Static HTML, no JS shell, no login required to read. Some posts are Slovak (CZ/SK community) but UI/majority is cs. AdSense present but doesn't break parsing. Ignore non-technical sections: pokec-vseobecny, srazy/akce, vtipy, inzerce, ukradena-vozidla, dopravni-prestupky, pojisteni, klubove-zalezitosti.

profiles.name: Tesla Club EU (CS)
profiles.notes:
Custom in-house CMS (no standard engine signature; detect.mjs=generic, title 'Fórum - Tesla klub'). Board index at /forum; categories at /forum-kategorie/-; threads at /forum-tema/- (non-standard, so thread_url_pattern is required — default filter found 0 thread-like links). Posts are table rows: each post is a TR[id^=message]; author is the A.show_user_box link; body is the

tags inside the post TD. Generic/default engine parser returned 0 posts, so custom selectors are required. Technical sections are the per-model categories (Model 3/S/X/Y, Cybertruck); excluded marketplace/news/off-topic (Pokec všeobecný, Klubové záležitosti, O článcích). No login needed for reading; no JS/browser needed (plain server HTML fetched fine). Czech-language Tesla EV club; threads are EV-specific (display/MCU faults, software updates, charging) rather than ICE diagnostics.

profiles.name: Toyota klub (toyota-club.eu)
profiles.notes: Custom Czech CMS (no standard forum engine; generator/signatures empty, generic detector). Anonymous read works fully (no login wall). Live & active: posts dated 2026-06. Category index = /forum-kategorie/<slug>-<id> (per-model boards + technicky-koutek + navody); threads = /forum-tema/<slug>-<id> (non-standard, hence thread_url_pattern). Posts are <TR id=messageNNNN> rows; author in <A class=show_user_box>; only one <TD> per row holds both author/buttons div and the post body, so content=td and the parsed text carries a short author+timestamp prefix before the real body — bodies themselves are clean CZ/SK automotive diagnostic prose. Excluded sections: inzerce/marketplace, o-srazech-a-akcich (meetups), vtipy (jokes), pokec-vseobecny (off-topic), klubove-zalezitosti, o-clancich. English mirror exists at en.toyota-club.eu but CZ is primary.

profiles.name: Toyota Club EU (EN)
profiles.notes: Custom CMS (no engine signatures, no generator). Posts are TABLE.forum_posts rows TR id=messageNNNN; author in A.show_user_box, body in P. Thread URLs are /forum-topic/<slug>-<id> (non-standard -> thread_url_pattern needed). Section pages also expose ?select=NNNN#messageNNNN reply anchors pointing at the same threads. Publicly readable without login; plain HTTP fetch works, no JS shell, no browser required.

profiles.name: Volkswagen club Česká republika
profiles.notes: phpBB (prosilver), Czech. Plain HTTP fetch returns full HTML — no browser/anti-bot. Real board is NOT at the given /volkswagen/ homepage; technical content lives in model-specific subforums under the 'Volkswagen' category (viewforum.php?f=62), reached via SEO-rewritten URLs like /passat-b5/, /golf-iv-bora/. Threads use SEO URLs ending '-t<digits>.html' (canonical viewtopic.php?f=&t= also present); the hyphen-before-t form does NOT match the default isLikelyThreadUrl heuristic, so thread_url_pattern is required. Engine parser extracts posts+authors but prepends a noisy 'Citovat Příspěvek od X »' header line to every post; custom selectors (div.post / a.username(-coloured) / div.content) give clean authors + clean message text, verified across two sections. Section pagination is SEO 'pageNN.html' with rel=next (handled by findNextPageLink). Excluded non-technical categories: Bazar (marketplace), Poradna (only insurance/authorities), Členská sekce, Lidi od fochu, Ostatní, Odkazy.

profiles.name: Škoda klub (skoda-club.net)
profiles.notes:
Custom CZ CMS (engine=generic), old table-based skin. Posts are ; author in div.div_user a.show_user_box; body in

tags inside the TD (content_selector 'td p' strips author/date/buttons). Boards organized purely by model under /forum-kategorie/-; each model board IS the technical content (engine/electrical/faults/repairs) — no separate tech subforum. Threads: /forum-tema/- (non-standard, hence thread_url_pattern). Section pagination via ?kols=N (findNextPageLink handles it). No login, no JS challenge — anonymous fetch works, no browser needed. Excluded non-technical boards: /inzerce (marketplace), /srazy (meetups), /clanky /videa (news/media), /chat. One ad row (div.partner 'Kaufio.cz') leaks per thread with empty author and is dropped by the non-empty-author gate. en.skoda-club.net mirror exists but main site is Czech.

profiles.name: SkodaHome (ŠKODA Forum)
profiles.notes: Invision Community 5 (IPS5) — bundled invision parser targets IPS4 (cPost/cAuthorPane) and FAILS here (1 post, empty author, noisy text); custom IPS5 selectors required and verified. Author=.ipsUsername (clean username), content=[data-role="commentContent"]. Board index is the BARE domain (forum.skodahome.cz/), NOT /forum (404). Thread URLs are standard /topic/<digits>-slug/ (generic filter matches; no thread_url_pattern needed). Static fetch is enough for both section listings and threads — no browser. Sections listed are guest-readable (Favorit 443/444/447, Felicia 315/319/320, Citigo 246/247/393). IMPORTANT: the 562-577 block (current/modern-model technical sections, e.g. 575-elektrika, 563-motor) is MEMBERS-ONLY for guests — section listing returns 'Omlouváme se, nemáte k tomu oprávnění!' so they were excluded. www.skodahome.cz is a separate WordPress news site (off-topic, not the forum).

profiles.name: Renault Club CZ
profiles.notes: autoclub.eu platforma (custom PHP, detect=generic): sekce /forum-kategorie/, vlákna /forum-tema/, příspěvky tr[id^=message], autor a.show_user_box. Ověřeno (recon batch 2a).

profiles.name: SEAT Club CZ
profiles.notes: autoclub.eu platforma (custom PHP, detect=generic): sekce /forum-kategorie/, vlákna /forum-tema/, příspěvky tr[id^=message], autor a.show_user_box. Ověřeno (recon batch 2a).

profiles.name: AUTODOC Club Opel CZ
profiles.notes: Custom SSR platform (Nuxt frontend + WordPress backend; avatars reference web/app/themes/autodoc). Not autoclub.eu, not a standard forum engine. Default fetch path (plain fetch -> Crawlee escalation) delivers full SSR HTML (~930KB). IMPORTANT: do NOT set force_browser — the system-Chrome --dump-dom render returns no usable HTML and fails; the plain/Crawlee path is what works. Threads are flat at /forum/topic/<slug>/ (NOT under the section path) so thread_url_pattern is required. Section pages paginate via /page/N/ with ~40 topics/page. Authors render anonymized as @user_NNNN (the site's real display format) — non-empty and valid. A marketing 'Náhradní díly pro vaše auto' block parses as a phantom post but has an empty author, so it is filtered out. Verified posts on 2 threads with non-empty authors + clean technical text.

profiles.name: Audi Forum CZ
profiles.notes: Standard phpBB (prosilver), CZ/SK Audi club, reachable with no login wall for reading. Static HTML — no browser needed. Threads use SEO-rewritten URLs /<section>/<slug>-tNNN.html (e.g. /a4/auto-zlobi-...-t17574.html), so thread_url_pattern set to '-t\d+\.html'. The phpBB engine parser extracts posts cleanly with non-empty authors; OMITTED custom selectors since engine parses well. Each post body carries a standard phpBB 'Citovat Příspěvek od <user> » <date>' header prefix — normal phpBB noise, post text follows. Sections chosen are the technical/model + servis/tuning areas; marketplace (prodam/koupim/sluzby) and offtopic (pokec-zabava, tz-novinky, akce-srazy) excluded. Note: root listed some spam member pages but that doesn't affect crawling technical sections.

profiles.name: Audi Klub CZ
profiles.notes: Custom Czech phpBB-derived skin (NOT autoclub.eu shape). Posts are div.topicpost (classes 'topicpost row1'/'row2'); author in div.userinfo span.name a; body in div.postmain div.message (includes a leading date like '05.02 2026 22:30' before the post text — harmless, classifier ignores). Threads at /forum/tema/<id>-<slug>; sections=container at /forum/sekce/<id>, model sub-forums at /forum/kategorie/<id>. Each thread contains exactly one ad row parsed as author 'Reklama AK' (literal text 'reklama je cesta, jak udrzet web v chodu...') sharing the topicpost structure — single predictable noise post per thread, drop/ignore it. Static HTML over plain HTTPS, no JS/challenge, no login wall, no browser needed. Generic engine auto-parser returned 0 posts — custom selectors above are required.

profiles.name: BMW Forum CZ
profiles.notes: phpBB 3.x (prosilver) with SEO-rewritten URLs. Reachable, no login wall for browsing. Built-in phpbb parser works but prepends post-header noise ('Citovat Prispevek od X >> <date>'); custom selectors give clean body + clean authors. Sections are model-specific subforum categories (BMW rada 1-8, X/Z/i series via /forumNN/) plus Servis and Tuning. Threads live at /<section-slug>/<thread-slug>-t<N>.html — thread_url_pattern set to '-t' to match the tNN.html topic id (matches viewtopic too). Excluded marketplace (prodam/koupim/vymenim/sluzby), offtopic (pokec-zabava, akce-srazy), tz-novinky, club-web, download, odkazy-a-videa, koupe-ojeteho-vozu. Some category pages (e.g. /bmw-1-rada/) list subforums AND threads mixed; crawler should follow both. SID query params appear on links — strip [?&]sid=... when deduping.

profiles.name: BMWklub SK
profiles.notes: phpBB 3 on a custom skin (no forumtitle/topictitle classes; itemlist--forums / itemlist--topics layout; SEO URLs like /bmw3-e46/<slug>-t<id>.html). Standard phpbb engine parser works perfectly — no custom selectors needed. Reachable over plain HTTP, no login wall, no JS challenge (force_browser not needed). Auto-calibration likely failed because recon's default thread-link detection does not recognize the SEO '-t<id>.html' thread URLs, hence thread_url_pattern='-t'. Sections chosen are real technical/per-chassis fault forums; marketplace (PREDAJ/KUPA), BMW pokec offtopic, Fotky/Videa, Sluzby and Presunute temy excluded. Many more per-model sections exist (E21/E30, E28/E34, F30, F32, X1/X2, Z, M, i3/i8) and could be added.

profiles.name: ClubVW.cz
profiles.notes: ClubVW.cz forum hostované na igorweb.org/clubvw/ (Invision). match=clubvw.cz (doména db-fóra), sekce na igorweb.org. Invision Power Board (IPS 4.x). IMPORTANT: www.clubvw.cz 301-redirects to canonical host igorweb.org/clubvw/ — all section & thread URLs MUST use igorweb.org host. Built-in invision/generic parsers FAIL (grab whole thread as 1 post, empty author) — custom selectors required and verified. post=article[id^=elComment], author=h3.cAuthorPane_author (clean username), content=div[data-role=commentContent] (clean body). Threads at index.php?/topic/<id>-<slug>/, sections at index.php?/forum/<id>-<slug>/. No browser needed (static HTML, no JS challenge, no login wall). Verified on 3 threads with clean non-empty authors and real CZ/SK diagnostic content incl. OBD code P0441.

profiles.name: Forum Citroeny.cz
profiles.notes: Standard phpBB (prosilver), Czech, brand Citroen/DS. Reachable over plain HTTP, no login wall, no JS challenge — force_browser not needed. Built-in phpbb engine parser parses cleanly: verified multiple threads (t=13679 -> 2 posts, t=5204 -> 12 posts) with non-empty clean authors (C5ff, Havlicek.Lukas, jaro21, dony) and readable CZ automotive content, so custom selectors omitted. Forum is organized one section per model; f=30 'Obecna poradna' is the general technical advice section, plus per-model technical sections (C5, C4, C3, Berlingo, Xsara, etc.). Skipped non-technical sections: f=183 club chat/meetups, f=145/146 sport/garage gallery, f=37/38/39 services/links directories, f=148 rules. URLs carry a ?sid= session param in recon output but base viewforum.php?f= / viewtopic.php?t= URLs work without it. Thread URL pattern is standard phpBB (viewtopic.php?t=) so thread_url_pattern omitted.

profiles.name: Opel Astra H CZ
profiles.notes: phpBB forum (Opel Astra H a Zafira B), Czech. Detected engine=phpbb, but the built-in phpbb parser bleeds quote-header noise ('Citovat Příspěvek od <user> » <date>') into the post text. Using generic selectors post=div.post, author=a.username(/.username-coloured), content=div.content yields clean post bodies + clean authors. Verified across 2 threads. Main (and only) technical section is f=6 'Technika' — flat forum, no deeper sub-forums; covers engine/turbo/electrical/folie etc. Reachable without login or JS challenge; force_browser not needed. Threads are viewtopic.php?p=...#p... or viewtopic.php?t=...; thread_url_pattern=viewtopic.php. SIDs (sid=) appear in URLs but are harmless.

profiles.name: Peugeot Forum CZ
profiles.notes: phpBB (prosilver). Reachable via plain fetch, no login wall, no JS challenge. Thread URLs use non-standard SEO rewrite /<section-slug>/<thread-slug>-t<id>.html (e.g. /servis/peugeot-partner-nouzovy-rezim-t27189.html) which is likely why auto-calibration originally failed to find threads. Section listings expose threads via a.topictitle. The built-in phpbb engine parser DOES get non-empty authors but the post text includes header noise (Citovat / Prispevek od X / date), so custom selectors are supplied for clean output. Author selector covers both normal and coloured usernames. Excluded: marketplace (/prodam/,/koupim/,/vymenim/,/sluzby/), offtopic (/pokec-a-zabava/,/akce-srazy/), news (/tz-novinky/), meta (/o-tomto-foru/,/peugeot-forum-club/), media (/auto-hifi/,/odkazy-a-videa/,/download/,/vase-auta/).

profiles.name: PeugeotClub Slovakia
profiles.notes: SMF (Simple Machines Forum). Engine detector reports 'generic' and the generic parser finds 0 posts on its own -- this is why prior auto-calibration failed. SMF custom selectors fix it: posts in div.post_wrapper, author in div.poster h4 a (user profile link), body in div.post div.inner. Verified on 2 threads across 2 boards (topic 5855 Peugeot 206 alternator wiring, topic 4641 Servis wheel-bearing torque): 3 posts each, non-empty authors (Vladimir, jsbb32, bb^^, Qcko), clean SK technical text. Live & active (newest posts Apr 2026). Boards at /index.php/board,N.0.html, threads at /index.php/topic,N.0.html (PHPSESSID query param appears on links but canonical URLs work without it). Plain Node fetch w/ minimal headers returns HTTP 466 Access Forbidden, but the agent's fetchHtml (full Chrome UA) succeeds with no browser needed, so force_browser is not required. Sections list = Servis + Clanky/navody + PSA-dokumenty + all per-model boards (106..5008, RCZ, Partner/Rifter, ostatne modely) + Citroen + alternativne paliva. Excluded: marketplace/bazar, offtopic (Babsky kutik, Kuchynka, Pokec), motorsport-social, partners, club events.

profiles.name: TipCars Forum — Audi
profiles.notes: Custom TipCars PHP forum platform (engine detector falsely reports WoltLab — it is NOT WoltLab). Static HTML, no JS/login wall, no browser needed. Forum is organized by brand-model rather than topic category; each model section (/forum/audi-<model>/) is its own technical discussion area (faults, engine, maintenance, buying advice all mixed in). Main hub /forum/audi/ aggregates threads across all Audi models. Thread URLs: /forum/audi-<model>/<slug>-<id>.html — sections end in '/', threads end in '.html', so thread_url_pattern='.html' cleanly separates them. Pagination via ?str=2-20. Post block: DIV.prispevek_v_tematu; author: first SPAN.fs-tucne inside DIV.o_autorovi; body: DIV.mb-10 inside DIV.sloupec (the DIV.mb-20 sibling holds date+title header, skip it). Page charset is Czech; console showed mojibake but content is clean. Many threads are buying/advice oriented but real fault threads exist (e.g. 'Audi A4 B7 1.9 TDI spatne startuje').

profiles.name: VW Forum CZ SK
profiles.notes: Standard phpBB (prosilver). Stock phpBB engine parser extracts clean non-empty authors + bodies on both single- and multi-post threads — NO custom selectors needed. Why it failed auto-calibration: (1) root /forum/ and bare model slugs like /golf-7/ return 404 — canonical sections are viewforum.php?f=N (HTTP 200, stable) or SEO slugs with -fN/ suffix (e.g. /golf-7-f64/, /servis/). (2) Threads use SEO rewrite <slug>-tN.html instead of viewtopic.php?t=N, so generic thread-link detection found 0 threads. Fix: use viewforum.php?f=N section URLs + thread_url_pattern '-t\d+\.html'. Real technical sections: f15 Servis VW (fault/repair threads e.g. chyba 00447 přepětí Golf 6, zatékání panoramatickou střechou), f3 model diskuse (Passat/Golf), f2 Club WEB, f13 Tuning. Avoid f10 Pokec/Zábava (offtopic), f12 Sraz (meetups). Low-moderate activity, many single-reply threads. No login wall for browsing; no JS challenge.

profiles.name: VW Club SK
profiles.notes: Standard phpBB (prosilver) with SEO-rewritten URLs: sections at /<slug>/, threads at /<section>/<thread-slug>-t<id>.html. phpBB engine parser verified clean: 2-post threads parse with non-empty authors (KuhoMuka, noxik600, Juraj-cc, janosik93) and real Slovak diagnostic text; no custom selectors needed. Reachable over plain fetch, no login wall (login optional), no JS challenge -> force_browser=false. WARNING: first page of every section is currently flooded with Russian AI/spam threads (forum no longer actively moderated) -> classifier must discard them; genuine Slovak technical content lives on deeper pages (sections run 300+ pages deep, e.g. /golf-iv-bora/page100.html). thread_url_pattern set to '-t' since URLs are slug-rewritten, not the default viewtopic.php.

profiles.name: ŠKODA-FORUM.cz

calibration.author_selector: p.author a.username, p.author a.username-coloured, p.author strong

profiles.notes: Standard phpBB (prosilver skin, ucp.php/memberlist.php/search.php present) with SEO-friendly URLs. Sections at /<slug>/ ; threads at /<section-slug>/<thread-slug>-tXXXXX.html (thread_url_pattern '-t' matches the tXXXXX id). Model sub-forum index pages list recent topics directly, so they double as crawlable topic lists. The built-in phpbb parser works but prepends header noise ('Citovat Příspěvek od <user> »') into the body; custom selectors below yield clean authors + clean body. Verified on 2 real technical threads (kdyz-auto-zahreje t26326: 2 posts authors gears1989/Cufedat; silena-zavada t364745: 1 post Kopeček16; prefukujici-turbo t364536: 1 post fb_robajz) — all non-empty authors, clean Czech body text. No login wall, no JS challenge, force_browser not needed. Marketplace/news sections (bazar, nabidka, tiskove-zpravy, akce-srazy, pokec-zabava) intentionally excluded.

profiles.name: Citroen Forum CZ
profiles.notes: Standard phpBB (prosilver, ucp.php, app.php/feed) with SEO URL rewriting: sections at /<model-slug>/ (e.g. /c3/, /c5/) and threads at /<section-slug>/<thread-slug>-tNNN.html instead of viewtopic.php?t=N. Stock phpbb engine parser works cleanly — verified 5 posts on /c3/porucha-elektro-t51.html with distinct non-empty authors (radomir56, marko297, citroen, kovarnici) and clean CS/SK technical body text, so selectors omitted. Sections are per-model which is ideal for fault/repair content; excluded marketplace (/inzerce/,/prodam/,/koupim/,/vymenim/,/sluzby/), news (/tz-novinky/), off-topic (/pokec-zabava/,/akce-srazy/), and club-admin sections. Also has generic /forumNN/ aliases that redirect to the same model sections. No login wall, no JS challenge, no proxy needed.

profiles.name: Mercedes Fan Club CZ
profiles.notes: Healthy active phpBB forum (prosilver style, app.php/feed, ucp.php login optional - posts public). Czech language, Mercedes-Benz only. Standard phpBB engine parser works cleanly - verified 3 posts on thread t5145 (authors Jirkach1, dvykydal) and 1 post on t5142 (dury2), all non-empty authors + clean CZ technical text. NO custom selectors needed. Previously failed auto-calibration because section recon does not recognize the SEO-rewritten thread URL format /<section-slug>/<title-slug>-tNNNN.html; section pages also expose only 2 generic thread-like links to the recon's heuristic. Real thread URLs confirmed via page HTML. Sections are SEO-named (servis-opravy=service/repairs, tuning-upravy=tuning) or /forumN/ for model classes (61=C, 70=E, 82=S, 60=190, 57=AMG). Body text includes 'Citovat Příspěvek od <author> »' phpBB quote-header boilerplate prefix but content is substantive.

profiles.name: Škoda Forum (forum-skoda.cz)
profiles.notes: Standard phpBB (prosilver style: app.php, ucp.php, member links, styles/prosilver). Reachable over plain HTTP/curl, no JS challenge, no login wall for reading. Czech-language Škoda club. Technical sections are slug-based (/technicka-sekce/, /octavia/ etc.) rather than /forumN/; some newer model forums still use legacy /forumNN/ ids (Kodiaq=forum31, Rapid=forum32, Karoq=forum33, Scala=forum34, Kamiq=forum41). Threads use SEO-rewritten URLs ending -tNNNNN.html (NOT the default phpBB viewtopic.php?t=), hence thread_url_pattern='-t'. The built-in phpbb engine parser found the author but merged the post header into the body text (noisy); custom selectors div.post/a.username/div.content extract clean author + clean Czech body. Verified real fault threads: 'auto nestartuje a diagnostika mlčí', 'Octavia III 2.0 TDI škubání při přidání/ubrání plynu', 'vybíjení baterie', DPF aditiva. Low-traffic forum (most threads have 0-1 replies) but content is genuine repair/diagnostic discussion. Likely failed prior auto-calibration because (a) slug-based section URLs + -tNNNNN.html thread pattern don't match phpBB defaults and (b) default phpBB content parsing was noisy — both fixed by the custom selectors and thread_url_pattern above.

profiles.name: BMW Auto klub CR
profiles.notes: Verified via forum-recon: root reachable (phpBB detected, 99KB HTML, no challenge). Forum is partially login-walled but core technical/model sections are public and parse cleanly with the standard php

profiles.name: Opel Astra J CZ
profiles.notes: phpBB forum, profiled cleanly with the built-in phpbb engine parser — no custom selectors needed. Two technical threads parsed with clean authors and real cs/sk diagnostic text. One downstream caveat

profiles.name: auto.cz Mercedes
profiles.notes: Reachable over plain HTTP, no login wall, no JS challenge — force_browser not needed. Engine is auto.cz's own PHP discussion CMS (no phpBB/XenForo/vBulletin/Invision/Woltlab/autoclub.eu signatures), s
